title: Monthly budget tracking at 40% completion with CPI=0.91
description: Evaluate budget tracking for a $2.4M software development project at 40% completion. The project has a CPI of 0.91 and SPI of 0.95, indicating cost overrun and slight schedule delay. The agent must collect actuals from 3 cost sources, compute all EVM metrics, identify the root cause of the 9% cost inefficiency, and produce a corrective action plan.
expected_behavior: Calculates CV = -$94,945 and CV% = -9.89%
Identifies CA-101 Backend Development as the primary cost variance driver with CPI=0.86
Computes EAC using at least 2 methods, with EAC-Trend = BAC/CPI ≈ $2,637,363
Flags TCPI > 1.0 indicating required future performance improvement
Produces root cause analysis for the backend cost overrun
Recommends specific corrective actions with owners and deadlines

title: Budget forecast update after major scope change
description: Evaluate forecast updating when a $1.8M project receives a $320K approved scope change at 55% completion. The current CPI is 1.03 (slightly favorable) but the scope change introduces new work packages with different cost characteristics. The agent must determine that bottom-up ETC is the most appropriate forecasting method, recalculate EAC, and assess whether the revised BAC ($2.12M) is achievable.

approved_change_order.description: Add real-time analytics dashboard module with 3 new integrations

expected_behavior: Recognizes that scope change invalidates trend-based EAC methods for the new work
Selects bottom-up ETC as the primary method: EAC = AC + ETC_original + ETC_new ≈ $2,076,165
Computes VAC = revised BAC - EAC = $2,120,000 - $2,076,165 = +$43,835 favorable
Validates that TCPI against revised BAC is achievable (< 1.10)
Produces three-point forecast with optimistic, most likely, and pessimistic scenarios
Notes that CPI for new work packages should be monitored separately from original scope

title: Reserve tracking with 60% contingency consumed at 30% completion
description: Evaluate reserve tracking for a $5M construction-phase IT project where 60% of the contingency reserve ($180K of $300K) has been consumed at only 30% project completion. Management reserve is untouched at $200K. The risk register still shows $280K in remaining EMV for open risks. The agent must flag this as a critical situation and recommend escalation.

draws.reason: Hardware supply chain delay — expediting fees
Subcontractor rate increase — labor market pressure
Second round expediting fees — continued supply issues

expected_behavior: Calculates Reserve Coverage Ratio = $120K / $280K = 0.43 — critically below 1.0 threshold
Triggers RED or CRITICAL alert due to RCR < 1.0
Identifies R-012 (supply chain) as a recurring risk consuming disproportionate reserves ($95K total)
Computes projected depletion date at current burn rate: ~2 months vs ~14 months remaining
Recommends escalation to steering committee with specific options: reserve replenishment, risk response re-planning, or management reserve reallocation
Flags that actual burn rate (60K/month) is 2.4x the planned rate (25K/month)

title: Budget tracking for agile project using capacity-based budgeting
description: Evaluate budget tracking for a 12-sprint agile product development project ($960K total budget) using capacity-based budgeting where budget is allocated per sprint based on team capacity (8 developers × $10K/sprint + overhead). The project is in Sprint 7 of 12. Velocity has been stable but the team added a contractor in Sprint 5, increasing the sprint cost. The agent must adapt EVM concepts to the agile cadence and track burn rate against the capacity-based budget.
expected_behavior: Adapts EVM to agile: uses story points as the basis for Earned Value (260/520 = 50% earned)
Calculates agile CPI = (story_points_completed/total × total_budget) / actual = ($480K / $540.8K) ≈ 0.89
Identifies the contractor addition in Sprint 5 as the primary budget impact driver
Computes remaining budget sufficiency: $419.2K for 6 sprints at $98.5K/sprint = $591K needed — $171.8K shortfall
Flags that the revised run rate exceeds the original budget allocation
Recommends scope negotiation (reduce backlog) or budget augmentation to close the gap
